abstract: This research evaluates the software quality of the e-learning system at Universitas Mahakarya Asia (e-learning.unmaha.ac.id) using the McCall Method. To provide a holistic view of the platform’s performance, the study assesses several key metrics: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability. The evaluation yielded an overall quality score of 79.6%, placing the system in the "Good" category. While the results demonstrate high performance in terms of system integrity and reliability, the study identifies specific weaknesses in information correctness and ease of maintenance. Based on these findings, we offer targeted recommendations to improve content accuracy, refine the user interface for a more intuitive experience, and streamline backend maintainability to ensure long-term sustainability. These insights serve as a strategic foundation for developing a more responsive and effective e-learning environment, further strengthening Universitas Mahakarya Asia’s commitment to high-quality digital education.
